c22570.st: and an apostle of Jesus Christ

c22571.st: in service of [the] faith of God᾽s elect

c22572.st: and [of their] knowledge of [the] truth that leads to godliness,

c22574.st: which God, who cannot lie, promised before time began.

c22575.st: [In His] own time, He has revealed His message in [the] proclamation

c22576.st: entrusted to me by [the] command of God our Savior.

c22577.st: To Titus, [my] true child in [our] common faith:

c22578.st: Grace and peace from God [the] Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.

c22579.st: The reason I left you in Crete

c22580.st: [was] that you would set in order what was unfinished

c22581.st: and appoint elders in every town,

c22583.st: [An elder must] be blameless, [the] husband of one wife,

c22585.st: [and are] not open to accusation of indiscretion or insubordination.

c22586.st: As God᾽s steward, an overseer must be above reproach —

c22587.st: not self-absorbed, not quick-tempered, not given to drunkenness,

c22588.st: not violent, not greedy for money.

c22589.st: Instead, [he must be] hospitable, a lover of good, self-controlled, upright, holy, [and] disciplined.

c22590.st: He must hold firmly to the trustworthy message as it was taught,

c22591.st: so that ... he will be able

c22593.st: and refute those who contradict [this message].

c22594.st: For many are rebellious and full of empty talk and deception,

c22598.st: For [the] sake of dishonorable gain, ... [and] teach things they should not.

c22599.st: [As] one of their own prophets has said,

c22600.st: “Cretans [are] always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”

c22603.st: so that they will be sound in the faith

c22604.st: [and] will pay no attention to Jewish myths [or] [to the] commands of men

c22606.st: To the pure, all things [are] pure;

c22607.st: but to the defiled and unbelieving, nothing [is] pure.

c22608.st: Indeed, both their minds and [their] consciences are defiled.

c22612.st: and unfit for any good deed.

c22613.st: But [as for] you, speak the things that are consistent with sound doctrine.

c22614.st: Older [men] are to be temperate, dignified, self-controlled,

c22615.st: [and] sound in faith, love, and perseverance.

c22616.st: Older [women], likewise, are to be reverent in [their] behavior,

c22617.st: not slanderers [or] addicted to much wine,

c22619.st: In this way they can train the young [women]

c22622.st: [to be] self-controlled, pure, managers of their households, kind,

c22624.st: so that the word of God will not be discredited.

c22625.st: In the same way, urge the younger [men] to be self-controlled. In everything,

c22626.st: show yourself {to be} an example [by doing] good works.

c22629.st: so that anyone who opposes [us] will be ashamed

c22630.st: to have nothing bad to say about us.

c22631.st: Slaves are to submit to their own masters in everything,

c22635.st: so that ... the teaching about God our Savior.

c22636.st: in every respect they will adorn

c22637.st: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men.

c22639.st: to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions,

c22640.st: [and] to live sensible, upright, and godly [lives] in the present age,

c22641.st: as we await the blessed hope and ... appearance

c22642.st: glorious ... of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.

c22644.st: to redeem us from all lawlessness

c22645.st: and to purify for Himself a people for His own possession,

c22647.st: Speak these things [as] you encourage and rebuke with all authority.

c22650.st: to be subject to rulers [and] authorities,

c22656.st: For at one time we too were foolish, disobedient,

c22658.st: enslaved to all sorts of desires and pleasures,

c22661.st: But when the kindness ... and [His] love for mankind appeared,

c22663.st: not by [the] righteous deeds we had done,

c22664.st: He saved us, ... but according to His mercy,

c22667.st: [This is the Spirit] He poured out on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior,

c22668.st: so that, having been justified by [His] grace,

c22669.st: we would become heirs with [the] hope of eternal life.

c22671.st: And I want you ... these things,

c22674.st: those who have believed God ... to devote themselves to good deeds.

c22675.st: These things are excellent and profitable for the people.

c22676.st: But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, arguments, and quarrels about [the] Law,

c22677.st: because [these things] are pointless and worthless.

c22678.st: Reject a divisive man after a first and second admonition,

c22680.st: that such a man is corrupt

c22683.st: As soon as I send Artemas or Tychicus to you,

c22684.st: make every effort to come to me at Nicopolis,

c22685.st: because I have decided to winter there.

c22686.st: Do your best to equip Zenas the lawyer and Apollos,

c22687.st: so that they will have everything they need.

c22689.st: to devote themselves to good works in order to meet [the] pressing needs [of others],

c22690.st: so that they will not be unfruitful.

c22691.st: All who [are] with me send you greetings.

c22692.st: Greet those who love us in [the] faith.
